2604.24536.title: Generating Place-Based Compromises Between Two Points of View
2604.24536.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) excel academically but struggle with social intelligence tasks, such as creating good compromises. In this paper, we present methods for generating empathically neutral compromises between two opposing viewpoints. We first compared four different prompt engineering methods using Claude 3 Opus and a dataset of 2,400 contrasting views on shared places. A subset of the gen erated compromises was evaluated for acceptability in a 50-participant study. We found that the best method for generating compromises between two views used external empathic similarity between a compromise and each viewpoint as iterative feedback, outperforming stan dard Chain of Thought (CoT) reasoning. The results indicate that the use of empathic neutrality improves the acceptability of compromises. The dataset of generated compromises was then used to train two smaller foundation models via margin-based alignment of human preferences, improving efficiency and removing the need for empathy estimation during inference.
2604.24536.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在學術上表現優異，但在社交智慧任務上卻面臨挑戰，例如創造良好的妥協。在本文中，我們提出了在兩種對立觀點之間生成情感中立妥協的方法。我們首先比較了四種不同的提示工程方法，使用Claude 3 Opus和一個包含2,400個對立觀點的共享地點數據集。生成的妥協子集在一項包含50名參與者的研究中進行了可接受性評估。我們發現，生成兩種觀點之間妥協的最佳方法是使用妥協與每個觀點之間的外部情感相似性作為迭代反饋，超越了標準的思考鏈（CoT）推理。結果表明，使用情感中立性提高了妥協的可接受性。然後，生成的妥協數據集被用來通過基於邊際的人類偏好對齊訓練兩個較小的基礎模型，提高了效率並消除了推理過程中對情感估計的需求。
2604.24536.author: Sumanta Bhattacharyya et.al.
2604.24536.authors: Sumanta Bhattacharyya, Francine Chen, Scott Carter, Yan-Ying Chen, Tatiana Lau, Nayeli Suseth Bravo, Monica P. Van, Kate Sieck, Charlene C. Wu
